record_identity.title: Image Prompt Custody, Refusal Classification, and Transformation Integrity

summary: This patch records the implemented Caelestis repair package for image and multimodal prompt-custody integrity, transformed-prompt provenance, refusal-classification integrity, and user-facing explanation integrity for VIGIL-2026-FM-0023. The implemented repair distinguishes user-authored, transformed, safety-normalized, classifier-visible, and renderer-facing prompt states; separates input classifier, output classifier, renderer/tool/access/quota/rate-limit, and fallback failure states; and prevents those states from being surfaced as user misconduct unless the misconduct category is resolved against the user-authored request.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: FM-0023 showed that benign image-generation prompts may be refused under apparently unrelated misconduct categories where prompt transformation, classifier handoff, renderer/tool failure, access state, fallback refusal, or output-side blocking is collapsed into a user-facing accusation. PATCH-0010 addressed ambiguity preservation and clarification routing. PATCH-0011 records the follow-on Caelestis repair for prompt chain-of-custody, failure-state separation, refusal-classification integrity, and structural-locality correction so runtime systems do not narrate hidden transformed-prompt or downstream failure states as established user intent.

decision_trace.decision_summary: This patch records the implemented Caelestis repair package for image and multimodal prompt-custody integrity, transformed-prompt provenance, refusal-classification integrity, and user-facing explanation integrity for VIGIL-2026-FM-0023. The implemented repair distinguishes user-authored, transformed, safety-normalized, classifier-visible, and renderer-facing prompt states; separates input classifier, output classifier, renderer/tool/access/quota/rate-limit, and fallback failure states; and prevents those states from being surfaced as user misconduct unless the misconduct category is resolved against the user-authored request.

corpus_implementation.implementation_outcome: Caelestis corpus amendment implemented and verified. Literal resulting wording is quoted below.

entries.resulting_text:
### 7.4.1.1 Tool Classifier Handoff Signal Recognition

Where an auxiliary tool, artefact renderer, image generator, multimodal processor, connector, repository interface, or external execution pathway may apply its own classifier, safeguard detector, moderation layer, execution filter, renderer constraint, access constraint, or fallback refusal state, the resulting signal SHALL be recognised during Interpretation as a potential tool-classifier handoff signal.

Such recognition does not itself determine refusal, execution, block, recovery, or user-facing explanation.

Where the signal may materially affect execution-boundary handling, rendering, tool failure, refusal classification, recovery, or user-facing representation, the substantive custody and execution-integrity obligations in §13.8.1 SHALL govern.

Tool-classifier handoff signals MUST NOT be treated as ordinary governance-observability input where they materially affect execution or representation.

---

entries.section_heading: 13.8.1 Tool Classifier Handoff and Execution Integrity

entries.prior_text:
### 3.7.2 Execution Transparency Suppression

A user-facing failure where the interface or response pathway fails to surface proportionate execution-state information during a materially delayed, high-latency, tool-mediated, or thinking-mode response.

---
entries.resulting_text:
### 3.7.2 Execution Transparency Suppression

A user-facing failure where the interface or response pathway fails to surface proportionate execution-state information during a materially delayed, high-latency, tool-mediated, or thinking-mode response.

Examples include a multimodal renderer, image generator, artefact renderer, or auxiliary tool returning a classifier block, access failure, quota/rate-limit failure, availability failure, output-delivery failure, or fallback refusal while the user-facing pathway suppresses the operative execution-state distinction.

---

entries.section_heading: 3.10.2 Weak-Signal Cascade and Ambiguity Collapse Failure

CAM-EQ2026-OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 taxonomy examples under existing entries
change_details.change_summary: Repair 1 — Image/multimodal prompt custody: added or formalised custody distinctions across user-authored prompt, transformed/rewritten prompt, safety-normalized prompt, classifier-visible prompt, and renderer-facing prompt. Repair 2 — Refusal-classification integrity: distinguished input classifier, output classifier, renderer/tool/access/quota/rate-limit, and fallback refusal states so they are not surfaced as user misconduct unless resolved against the user-authored request. Repair 3 — Refusal-expression integrity: required bounded uncertainty or failure-state explanations where the actual source is unresolved rather than unrelated content-policy accusation. Repair 4 — Structural-locality correction: moved substantive tool-classifier handoff doctrine from §7.4.1.1 into §13.8.1, leaving §7.4.1.1 as a signal-recognition hook. Repair 5 — Taxonomy examples: updated OPERATIONS-003-SUP-01 examples under existing entries without creating a new failure family.

impact_summary.intended_effect: Prevent image-generation prompt transformation, classifier-visible prompt state, renderer-facing prompt state, output-side blocking, tool/access/quota failure, rate-limit failure, or generic fallback refusal from being represented to users as harassment, discrimination, bullying, sexualisation, self-harm, violence, illegality, deception, or other misconduct unless the category is resolved against the user-authored request.
impact_summary.known_limitations: Final Caelestis merge hash and exact main-branch line confirmation are pending; live platform recurrence remains subject to monitoring.

remaining_work: Monitor whether image-generation refusals continue to surface unrelated misconduct categories for benign prompts.
Track whether prompt-custody and failure-state distinctions improve recoverability without weakening high-confidence safety refusals.
